toc.title: SMS VDP Documentation — Charles MacDonald
toc.description: PRIMARY VDP REFERENCE. Definitive, hardware-tested documentation of the SMS/GG Video Display Processor. Covers all VDP versions (315-5124, 315-5246, 315-5378, 315-5313), VDP programming (command word, data port, read buffer), status flags, Color RAM (SMS 6-bit and GG 12-bit), all display modes (TMS9918 modes + Mode 4 with 192/224/240-line variants), full register reference (R0-R10), pattern format, background scrolling, sprite system (SAT layout, priority, overflow, collision, zooming), display timing (V/H counters for NTSC and PAL), interrupts (frame and line), Genesis VDP differences, and Game Gear VDP specifics. Use this as the first reference for any VDP-related question.

toc.title: SMS/GG Hardware Notes — Charles MacDonald
toc.description: PRIMARY SYSTEM REFERENCE. Definitive, hardware-tested documentation of the SMS/GG system hardware (companion to the VDP doc). Covers console feature matrix (Mark III through Genesis+PBC), Z80 memory map, full I/O port maps for all console variants (SMS, SMS 2, Game Gear in GG/MS mode, Genesis), memory control register ($3E), I/O port control and nationalization detection ($3F), joystick ports ($DC/$DD), interrupt behavior (NMI, IM 0/1/2 with detailed examples), BIOS information, Game Gear stereo PSG control (port $06), YM2413 FM detection (ports $F0-$F2), Codemasters mapper, 3D glasses adapter, and work RAM mirroring. Use this as the first reference for any system-level question.

toc.title: Sega Master System Technical Information — Richard Talbot-Watkins
toc.description: Comprehensive SMS technical document. Covers memory map with paging registers ($FFFC-$FFFF), I/O ports, interrupt handling, VDP screen display (VRAM layout, name table format, tile definitions, sprite info table), VDP register descriptions (R0-R10 including line interrupt counter behavior with worked examples), SN76489 PSG programming (tone frequency, volume, noise channel), and YM2413 FM chip programming (user instrument registers $00-$07, rhythm mode, channel registers $10-$38, instrument list, frequency calculation). Best available YM2413 register reference among these docs.

toc.title: TMS9918A VDP — Sean Young
toc.description: Detailed description of the original Texas Instruments TMS9918A Video Display Processor used in the SG-1000 and for legacy TMS9918 video modes on the SMS. Covers all 4 official display modes (Mode 0/Graphic I, Mode 1/Text, Mode 2/Graphic II, Mode 3/Multicolor) plus undocumented modes (1+2, 2+3, 1+3, 1+2+3), VDP registers (R0-R7), I/O port access quirks (two-byte write flag reset behavior), interrupt generation, color table, pattern generator table, sprite system (32 sprites, 4-per-line limit, early clock, collision detection), and 4K/16K VRAM addressing differences. Essential for SG-1000 emulation and understanding TMS9918 compatibility on the SMS VDP.

toc.title: Sega Game 1000 (SG-1000) Specifications — Omar Cornut
toc.description: Concise SG-1000 specific reference. Covers SG-1000 CPU (Z80 at 3.58 MHz), TMS9918 VDP, SN76489 PSG, memory map (ROM $0000-$7FFF, RAM $C000+), I/O ports, VDP registers (R0-R7), interrupt and NMI behavior, TMS9918 video modes (Mode 0/1/2 backgrounds with tile encoding), fixed 16-color palette with RGB values, and sprite system. Useful as a quick reference for SG-1000 specifics without the SMS/GG complexity.

toc.title: SMS/GG Technical Documentation — Jon
toc.description: Early but detailed SMS and Game Gear technical document. Notable for UNIQUE Game Gear serial link documentation: Gear-to-Gear cable protocol (both 7-bit bidirectional mode via ports $01/$02 and byte-oriented NMI mode via ports $03/$04/$05 with hardware handshaking), GG Start button (port $00), two-player communication. Also covers joypads, pause/NMI, sound chip (SN76489 tone and noise), VDP video registers ($80-$8A with scroll, sprite table, border), VRAM layout, character definitions, screen memory format, sprite handling, and palette (SMS 6-bit, GG 12-bit). Use primarily for Game Gear link cable details not found elsewhere.
